arts.sum: Team news confirmed from Anfield as Liverpool entertain Manchester United in the Premier League

arts.ttl: Team News: Slot, Amorim confirm XIs for Liverpool vs. Man United
arts.sum: Liverpool boss Arne Slot and Manchester United head coach Ruben Amorim confirm their starting sides for Sunday's Premier League clash at Anfield.

arts.ttl: Ugarte, Fernandes and Mainoo return for Man Utd vs Liverpool LIVE!
arts.sum: It is treacherous out there but the dangers for Manchester United are on the pitch this afternoon. They have scored in their last 23 matches under Arne Slot and Mohamed Salah, in supreme form right now, has destroyed better United teams than this one.

arts.ttl: How Man Utd would line up with Victor Osimhen
arts.sum: Signing at least one new wing-back is a priority for 2025, but Diogo Dalot and Noussair Mazraoui will keep their places for the time being, while Kobbie Mainoo and Manuel Ugarte will feature in central midfield. Leny Yoro is expected to grow into a key player at Old Trafford this year and he could feature alongside Lisandro Martinez and Harry Maguire in a back three system.

arts.ttl: Liverpool triple team news hint as Manchester United clues dropped
arts.sum: Arne Slot must make at least one change to the Liverpool starting line-up as Joe Gomez is injured, but Conor Bradley and Ibrahima Konate are available once again

arts.ttl: Liverpool v Manchester United: Premier League – live
arts.sum: Premier League updates from the 4.30pm GMT kick-off Fulham v Ipswich – live | Latest tables | And mail Simon This game got the go-ahead shortly after midday today after two meetings of Liverpool council’s safety advisory group, and despite a heavy overnight snowdump. “Seems a bit weird that this game has been given the go-ahead, but I guess it suits Liverpool down to the ground as United are in total disarray and this is as close to a home banker as you will ever see in the Premier League,” writes Rick Harris. It certainly suits Liverpool, both because of the two teams’ recent performances and the impact a second rescheduled game would have on their schedule later this season, though given there is a team in this season’s league that has lost eight of their 10 away games and won none of them, and this fixture’s propensity for surprises, I don’t think the result is a formality (the bookies have an away win at somewhere around 6/1, which (totally useless fact alert) is the same as Paul Mescal being named the next James Bond. Continue reading...

arts.ttl: Michael Owen’s response over if he his a 'Liverpool legend’ will rile Reds fans
arts.sum: Michael Owen left Liverpool for Real Madrid in 2004, but his controversial decision to join Manchester United five years later remains a sore subject for many Reds fans

arts.ttl: Jamie Carragher spots Liverpool's biggest problem against Man United
